t_ch: 你的举动让扑满很不满，它也该得到尊重。
t_en: The trotter is very displeased with what you have done. It deserves respect, too.

t_ch: （玩的差不多了）
t_en: (That's enough playing)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 扑满看上去还很精神。人不能把有限的精力投入到无限的玩弄扑满中去。
t_en: The trotter still looks full of energy, but people can't afford to put all of their limited energy into endless play sessions with trotters.

t_ch: 向它声明房间守则。
t_en: Declare the room rules to it.

t_ch: 这里是你的地盘，你的地盘，必须得听你的。
t_en: This is your territory. Your territory, and it must listen to you.

t_ch: 不准随意上床。
t_en: No jumping on the bed.

t_ch: 哼哼哼～唧！
t_en: Oink oink oink~ squeal!

t_ch: 它对「床」的兴趣不大。还是在地上打滚比较自由。
t_en: It doesn't seem very interested in the bed. Rolling around on the floor feels freer.

t_ch: 不准偷吃零食。
t_en: No stealing snacks.

t_ch: 哼～唧！
t_en: Oink~ squeal!

t_ch: 它的意思是：「就分我一口嘛！」
t_en: Its response means, "Just share a bite with me!"

t_ch: 不准夜间喧哗。
t_en: No loud noises at night.

t_ch: 哼…哼…哼……
t_en: Oink... Oink... Oink...

t_ch: 它小声应答。似乎在避免「喧哗」。
t_en: It answers softly, as if trying to avoid making noise.

t_ch: 不准偷玩电脑。
t_en: No playing with the computer.

t_ch: 哼哼，哼唧？
t_en: Oink oink, oink? Squeal?

t_ch: 它哪里会玩电脑啊！
t_en: As if it could play on the computer!

t_ch: 总之要听话！
t_en: In any case, it has to obey!

t_ch: 哼！
t_en: Oink!

t_ch: 它说：「遵命！」
t_en: It says, "At your command!"

t_ch: 对于一只自由自在的扑满来说，这些规则委实太严苛了。
t_en: For a free-spirited trotter, these rules are indeed too strict.

t_ch: 暂且请它出去。
t_en: For now, ask it to leave.

t_ch: 只有收到邀请函的客人才能进入你的私人空间！
t_en: Only guests with an invitation can enter your private space!

t_ch: 这沙发好软啊…感觉是个睡觉的好地方。
t_en: This sofa is so soft... It feels like a good place to sleep.

t_ch: 睡大觉！
t_en: Sleepy time!

t_ch: 嘿嘿，倒头就睡～
t_en: Hehe, just flop down and sleep~

t_ch: 还是别在这里睡了。
t_en: Maybe it's better not to sleep here.

t_ch: 怎么一觉醒来，我回到自己的房间了？
t_en: How did I wake up back in my room?

t_ch: 难道是梦游？
t_en: Could it be sleepwalking?

t_ch: 并不是梦游哦～有人小心翼翼地将你送了回来。
t_en: No, it's not sleepwalking — someone carefully brought you back.

t_ch: 是谁把我送回来的吗？
t_en: Someone brought me back?

t_ch: 没错，那人小心翼翼，生怕将你惊醒。
t_en: That's right. That person was cautious, afraid of waking you.

t_ch: 是哪位好心人送我回来的？
t_en: Which kind-hearted person brought me back?

t_ch: 这并不重要，因为列车组的每个人都会这样做。他们每个人都时刻关爱着你，也时刻关爱着彼此。
t_en: It doesn't matter, because everyone in the Express Crew would do the same. Each one cares for you and for each other.

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「造物引擎模型」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "Engine of Creation model."

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「穷观阵摆件」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "Matrix of Prescience ornament."

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「星槎航模」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "Model Starskiff."

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「枘凿六合便携版」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "Portable Hexanexus."

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「钟表小子闹钟」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "Clockie Alarm Clock."

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「{TEXTJOIN#87}模型」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "{TEXTJOIN#87} model."

t_ch: 你的目光扫过收藏架上的藏品，最后停留在「垃圾桶摆件」上。
t_en: As you cast your gaze across the collectibles on the display shelf, it lands on the "Trashcan ornament."

t_ch: （打开音乐播放器）
t_en: (Turn on the music player)

t_ch: 当星宿的回响散去，你的耳边接踵而来的是…浪潮的声音？
t_en: As the echoes of the constellations fall silent, the next sound to meet your ears is... waves?

t_ch: （泰坦的呓语）
t_en: (Titan's murmurs)

t_ch: 陌生的呼唤带着渴望，将你的神识引入池中……
t_en: The unfamiliar call carries a longing to take your soul's essence into the pool...

t_ch: 聆听星宿之声
t_en: Listen to the Voice of Constellation

t_ch: 进入潮汐的馈赠
t_en: Enter Tidal Bounty

t_ch: 离开
t_en: Leave

t_ch: 一个只有你我的地方…
t_en: A place for just the two of us...

t_ch: 宁芙，我的宁芙…
t_en: Nympha, my beloved Nympha...

t_ch: 我们离开奥赫玛吧…
t_en: Let's leave Okhema...

t_ch: 厄琉斯，我的厄琉斯…
t_en: Aelius, my cherished Aelius...

t_ch: 抵达了行进路线的下一站
t_en: You have arrived at the next step along your route

t_ch: 「奇迹宝珠」让此处的时间流向发生了改变，暂时无法开启追忆残像
t_en: Miracle Orbs have altered the flow of time in this area. Fragments of Recollection cannot be activated at the moment.

t_ch: 每一场伟大冒险的背后，都离不开大地兽的身影！你好，陌生人，看你的眼神，你也像我一样喜欢大地兽，对吗？
t_en: No grand adventure is complete without a dromas! Hey there, stranger. Judging by that glint in your eyes, you're a fan of dromas just like me, right?

t_ch: 我是伊阿宋，目前管理着这支商队「阿尔戈」。奥赫玛的大地兽都是我的好朋友，它们也是多亏了我才有自己的名字。
t_en: I'm Iason, the manager of this caravan "Argo." All dromas in Okhema are my good friends, and I'm the one who gave each of them a name.

t_ch: 大地兽的名字都是你取的？
t_en: You named all the dromases?

t_ch: 是啊。阿斯忒里翁、欧斐摩斯、卡斯托尔、波鲁克斯、还有最强壮的赫拉克勒斯——个顶个的好名字。
t_en: Exactly. Asterion, Euphemus, Castor, Pollux, and the mightiest of them all, Heracles — all solid names.

t_ch: 你这商队做什么生意？
t_en: What does your caravan trade in?

t_ch: 什么都做！「阿尔戈」原本只做大宗货物的运输，但现在如你所见，大家都不容易，我们也要尽己所能。
t_en: Everything! Argo used to just carry bulk goods, but times have been rough, so we've expanded our business.

t_ch: 最近世道挺乱，你要是有什么东西想从外面运进奥赫玛来，或者就是想买一些稀奇物件，找我们「阿尔戈」下单，那是最安全的。赫拉克勒斯像黄金裔一样值得信赖！
t_en: Things aren't as calm as they used to be. If you need to move anything into Okhema or find something rare, Argo's your safest bet. Trust me, Heracles is just as dependable as the Chrysos Heirs!

t_ch: 据说在遥远的黄金世，有一艘会飞的大船叫做阿尔戈，而他的船主也叫做伊阿宋。那位伊阿宋用飞船载着他的大地兽朋友纵横世界、见义勇为，就像今天的黄金裔一样。
t_en: Legend has it that in the Era Chrysea eons ago, there was this massive airship called Argo, whose captain was also named Iason. Iason sailed the world with his dromas buddies, helping out those in need, much like the Chrysos Heirs do these days.

t_ch: 朋友，看你的眼神就知道，你也向往着和大地兽伙伴一起冒险的生活，没错吧？
t_en: Friend, I can see that spark in your eyes — the yearning for adventure with your dromas buddies. Am I right?

t_ch: 你好像很懂大地兽？
t_en: You seem well-versed in dromas.

t_ch: 哎呀，这你可找对人了，那边工坊里的人都说我是「大地兽偶像制作人」，没有人比我更懂大地兽们的故事。你想听谁的？
t_en: You've found the dromas master! Down at the workshop, they all call me the Dromas Idol Producer! When it comes to those marvelous creatures, I'm your go-to person! Whose story catches your eye?

t_ch: 大地兽阿斯忒利翁
t_en: Let's talk about Asterion.

t_ch: 从{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}庇护下的第一个生命，到大地兽第一次行走世间，阿斯忒利翁已经经历许多。
t_en: Asterion has been on quite the journey, starting from the time they were chosen as the first living being under the protection of {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}, all the way to when the young dromas began exploring the world.

t_ch: 不幸的是，过去的伟大冒险也让它内心伤痕累累，患上了名为{RUBY_B#不愿见人}广场恐惧症{RUBY_E#}的焦虑障碍。
t_en: Unfortunately, those arduous adventures have taken their toll on Asterion, causing a deep anxiety disorder known as {RUBY_B#Reluctance to Meet People}agoraphobia{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 如果你看到它总是把头藏在{RUBY_B#兽舍}自家{RUBY_E#}的篷布里面，还请体量下一位迟暮英雄只愿安度晚年的心情。
t_en: If you ever catch Asterion seeking solace in their {RUBY_B#barn}sanctuary{RUBY_E#}, please remember they're just an old hero looking for a quiet retirement.

t_ch: 大地兽欧斐摩斯
t_en: Let's talk about Euphemus.

t_ch: 欧斐摩斯是阿斯忒利翁的战友，也是除了赫拉克勒斯之外最强壮的大地兽。欧斐摩斯曾跟随着我，在黑潮侵袭之前抢救出了科尔基斯的稀世珍宝——
t_en: Euphemus is a close comrade of Asterion's, and the mightiest dromas after Heracles. We once made it to Corchis just before the black tide hit and managed to salvage its legendary treasures...

t_ch: 四大箱的橡皮鸭子！
t_en: Four crates packed with rubber ducks!

t_ch: 唉，那一趟可实在点背，四个那么大的箱子，我还以为肯定装了什么金银财宝——结果价值还抵不过路途上的开销。
t_en: Yeah, that was an unlucky trip, I admit. I was hoping for some real treasures in those crates, but those rubber ducks barely covered our costs.

t_ch: 早知道应该就地打开那几个箱子，让橡皮鸭子们跟着黑潮随波逐流拉倒……
t_en: I should have just popped open those crates right there and let those ducks ride the black tide...

t_ch: 大地兽卡斯托尔、波鲁克斯
t_en: Let's talk about Castor and Pollux.

t_ch: 卡斯托尔和波鲁克斯是两头年轻的大地兽，卡斯托尔比波鲁克斯稍稍年长一点，它们都还不曾经历那些考验生死的冒险。
t_en: Castor and Pollux are a pair of young dromases. Castor is a bit older than Pollux, and neither has faced any life-or-death adventures yet.

t_ch: 不过，为了它们将来的生计着想，我拟了一份非常有吸引力的简历，可以把它们包装成一对前所未有的双胞胎组合！来来来，我念给你听听：
t_en: However, in preparation for their future business, I've concocted a cool pitch that could make them the ultimate duo! Let me share it with you!

t_ch: 受{RUBY_B#浪漫之泰坦}黄金之茧{RUBY_E#}的垂青，命运为我们带来了一对罕见的孪生大地兽——卡斯托尔与波鲁克斯！诞生自{RUBY_B#大地之泰坦}磐岩之脊{RUBY_E#}的它们拥有非凡的自信与默契，就像两位尚未接受洗礼但命中已注定成为英雄的黄金裔！
t_en: Through the {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Chrysalis of Gold{RUBY_E#}'s grace, we present to you a rare pair of twin dromases, Castor and Pollux! Hailing from {RUBY_B#Earth Titan}Georios{RUBY_E#}, these two boast impressive teamwork and confidence, much like two Chrysos Heirs gearing up for their heroic fate!

t_ch: …阿格莱雅大人应该不会把这当成虚假宣传吧？
t_en: ...Lady Aglaea won't consider this false advertising, right?

t_ch: 大地兽赫拉克勒斯
t_en: Let's talk about Heracles.

t_ch: 哈哈，你总算问到了，我最喜欢的赫拉克勒斯！听着，赫拉克勒斯不光是所有现役的大地兽里最强壮、最聪明的——就算是萨白尼从黑潮里复活，那里也找不到比它更好的大地兽～
t_en: Aha! You've finally brought up my favorite, Heracles! Let me tell you, he's not just the strongest and smartest dromas around today — Even if Sabany came back from the black tide, no dromas there would outshine him!

t_ch: 这些年来，赫拉克勒斯跟着我完成了无数不可能的任务，我挑选了其中最困难的十二次，将它们称为「赫拉克勒斯的十二试炼」。
t_en: Heracles has been my loyal partner through countless impossible tasks. Among them, I've dubbed the toughest twelve challenges he faced as The Twelve Trials of Heracles.

t_ch: 想了解一下吗？这是我给赫拉克勒斯专门撰写的故事，你也收一本吧。
t_en: Eager to hear more about his adventures? I've penned an epic just for Heracles. Here, take a copy.

t_ch: 行商不易，后会有期。
t_en: Wish you all the best in your business.

t_ch: 哈哈，借您吉言。改天说不定还指望着您照顾生意呢，再会啊～
t_en: Thank you! Feel free to swing by and grab anything that catches your eye! Catch you later!

t_ch: 行商不易，后会有期。
t_en: Wish you all the best in your business.

t_ch: 哈哈，借您吉言。改天说不定还指望着您照顾生意呢，再会啊～
t_en: Thank you! Feel free to swing by and grab anything that catches your eye! Catch you later!

t_ch: 看看你卖的稀奇物件。
t_en: What rare stuff do you have?

t_ch: 好嘞！不过你可别笑我，小店最近存货不多，仅剩的都是些大地兽纪念品了。
t_en: Feel free to pick out anything that catches your eye. Sadly, my stock's running low these days, and all I've got left are dromas souvenirs.

t_ch: 这位客人，最后一只黄金裔陶罐刚刚卖出。如果你是为此而来，对不住让你白跑一趟了！
t_en: The last Chrysos Heir dolium just got snatched up, dear customer. If that's what you came for, I'm sorry for the disappointment!

t_ch: 黄金裔陶罐是什么？
t_en: What is a "Chrysos Heir dolium"?

t_ch: 哈哈，看来是我误会您的来意了。只怪近期光顾小店的顾客十有八九都是冲着这个系列来的。
t_en: Haha, it looks like I misread your interest. Lately, it seems like everyone who stops by is on the hunt for that specific line of products.

t_ch: 还有这种创收项目？
t_en: Does such a business exist?

t_ch: 哈哈，看来是我误会您的来意了。只怪近期光顾小店的顾客十有八九都是冲着这个系列来的。
t_en: Haha, it looks like I misread your interest. Lately, it seems like everyone who stops by is on the hunt for that specific line of products.

t_ch: 我都预购十年了还没货！
t_en: I placed an order for a dolium ten years ago! And it still hasn't arrived!

t_ch: 向您再次致歉！尽管这店还没开过十年。近期光顾小店的顾客十有八九都是冲着这个系列来的，一直缺少库存。
t_en: My apologies once again! Even though my shop has been around for a decade, most recent visitors have been on the lookout for my Chrysos Heir Dolia, and I've never managed to keep enough in stock.

t_ch: 某日我望见在城里巡逻的白厄大人，灵光一闪将奥赫玛黄金裔们的英雄事迹绘制在了陶罐之上。
t_en: As for their origin, one day, while spotting Lord Phainon patrolling the city, I felt inspired and painted the epic tales of Okhema's Chrysos Heirs on a dolium.

t_ch: 大人们神武英勇，容貌悦人，定是「黄金之茧」墨涅塔的蝶翼扇动让鳞粉落在他们身上，才生出如此完美的人来！
t_en: Their courage and elegance must come from the fluttering wings of Mnestia, the Chrysalis of Gold! Their magical powder must have imbued them with such perfection!

t_ch: 我的陶罐自然也沾染了这份荣耀，如今每到发售日，黄金裔的崇拜者都要将我这小店围得水泄不通。
t_en: Naturally, my dolia carry this same honor. Now, fans of the Chrysos Heirs flock to my humble shop on sale days.

t_ch: 说实话，我还怪不习惯的，毕竟不久前我还是个穷困潦倒、无人理睬的街头陶罐小画家，终日闷在自己的世界，偶尔在那儿街对角的广场边上支个铺，画点陶罐卖来糊口。
t_en: Honestly, it's all a bit surreal to me. Not too long ago, I was still a struggling artist lost in my thoughts, setting up a booth on the plaza across the street, painting on dolia here and there to make ends meet.

t_ch: 这黄金裔陶罐卖脱销后，我店面开起来了，人也小有名气，明日我还要去云石天宫，为某位元老老爷的大寿作副画像。
t_en: After the frenzy over these Chrysos Heir Dolia, I opened this shop and gained some recognition. Tomorrow, I'm off to the Marmoreal Palace to paint a portrait for a distinguished elder's grand birthday bash!

t_ch: 只不过…总觉得少了点什么……
t_en: However... something feels off...

t_ch: 我懂你！
t_en: I get where that comes from!

t_ch: 想不到，您貌如初生的新月般年轻，竟对人生领悟如此透彻。
t_en: It's remarkable to see someone as young as a new crescent moon with such profound insights into life.

t_ch: 名利双收还不好？
t_en: Isn't it amazing to achieve both fame and fortune?

t_ch: 可不敢当。
t_en: Well, I'm nothing special.

t_ch: …您瞧，此刻{RUBY_B#五大时刻之三}明晰时{RUBY_E#}时光正好，就不提昨日苦闷啦。
t_en: ...Let's just bask in the bright light of the {RUBY_B#Third of the Five Moments}Lucid Hour{RUBY_E#} and forget the days of sorrow.

t_ch: 除了陶罐，我这儿还卖四处寻来的奇珍古玩，也是我的心头好，看到感兴趣的请和我讲。
t_en: Apart from the Dolia, I also offer a range of ancient artifacts I've gathered from all over. If anything piques your interest, just let me know.

t_ch: 手绘陶罐，奇珍古玩，价格不贵，随便看随便瞧。
t_en: Hand-painted pots and ancient artifacts, all at reasonable prices. Feel free to take a look around.

t_ch: 想和你再聊聊…
t_en: Let's talk about some other topics...

t_ch: 没问题，您想聊什么？
t_en: Of course! What's on your mind?

t_ch: 黄金裔陶罐是正版授权吗？
t_en: Are your Chrysos Heir Dolia officially licensed?

t_ch: 那当然，艺术家的版权意识可是很到位的。
t_en: Definitely. You know, artists take their copyrights seriously.

t_ch: 我挨个征得过他们的同意，我一度想放弃时，还是他们中的某位大人开导我不要为拥抱商业性而内疚自责。
t_en: I secured permission from each heir individually. Even when I had doubts about commercializing them, one wise heir actually reassured me not to feel guilty.

t_ch: 还有位颇为负责的大人，偶尔也会来与我探讨人体绘画之美妙哩，绘制肌肉线条如何流畅又饱满，哪个款式的肩甲能显得身形更加宽阔……
t_en: There's another responsible heir who drops by occasionally, discussing the beauty of portraying the human form, the intricate lines of muscles, and the armor styles that highlight the body's figure...

t_ch: 这是可以说的吗？
t_en: Is that something you can divulge?

t_ch: 无…无可奉告，我不会透露客户隐私的。
t_en: I... I'm sorry, but I won't delve into my customers' privacy.

t_ch: 我已经想到是谁了。
t_en: I think I can already guess who that is.

t_ch: 可…可不兴这么说，传出去以为我透露客户隐私了。
t_en: Please... don't say anything like that. If that gets out, people might think I've breached my customers' privacy.

t_ch: 为何选择在陶罐上作画？
t_en: Why did you choose to paint on dolia?

t_ch: 绘制陶罐是翁法罗斯最古老的记叙方式之一，我喜欢绘制古老的传说，还是要用最传统的方式才有岁月沉淀的韵味。
t_en: Because painting on dolia is one of the oldest storytelling traditions in Amphoreus. I prefer to depict ancient legends in the most traditional manner possible for that authentic touch.

t_ch: 况且陶器的器形多样，做各种用途的都有，能将画作悄然融入生活之中，比如城中不少奇美拉砂盆也出自我手。
t_en: Plus, pottery offers a canvas of diverse shapes and purposes, allowing me to infuse art into life. You know, I've even crafted many chimera litter trays in this city.

t_ch: 等千万年之后，说不定我的陶罐也会被后人当作古董文物好好研究一番，真期待见到那天呀！
t_en: Maybe my dolia will be studied as antiques and relics after thousands of years. If only I could witness that day myself!

t_ch: 要研究奇美拉砂盆吗……
t_en: Studying chimera litter trays...

t_ch: 哈哈哈！除了味儿有点大，审美一定经得起时间的考验。
t_en: Haha! My art will definitely withstand time, even if it comes with a bit of a stink.

t_ch: 翁法罗斯能撑到那天吗？
t_en: Will Amphoreus exist until then?

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯令时间的长河奔流不止，即使你我毁灭，也会浇灌出下一个文明。
t_en: Oronyx's grace keeps the river of time flowing. Even if we fade away, a new civilization will eventually bud again.

t_ch: 我当然还是希望翁法罗斯存续，这就要拜托各位黄金裔大人了。
t_en: But then again, I do hope Amphoreus endures — a responsibility that rests on all esteemed Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 关于你上次欲言又止…
t_en: About our unfinished conversation from last time...

t_ch: 我深知我的不知足，但…墨涅塔在上，描绘翁法罗斯的岁月才是我提笔的初衷——从已绝迹的飞狮走兽，到泰坦开天辟地的神话史诗，再到早已消亡的泰坦秘仪……
t_en: I know my limits, but... praise Mnestia, chronicling the years of Amphoreus is my original inspiration. I aim to portray everything related to it, from extinct manticores to the Titans' epics, and their long-lost rituals...

t_ch: 可承住我理想的创作，并非如今大家认可我的原因…人们只记得我常为奥赫玛的名流作画，也只爱我笔下的黄金裔。
t_en: But people do not currently acknowledge me due to the creations that bear my true dream... People remember me mostly for my paintings of the upper echelon of Okhema and my portrayal of the Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 的确，黄金裔的光辉拽起了灵感的丝线，我对他们的神性之美顶礼膜拜，但我不得不承认这其中掺有不少现实的考量……
t_en: While the allure of the Chrysos Heirs inspires me and I admire their divine beauty, I have to admit that practicality also played a role in my choice of clients...

t_ch: 这种错位，多少让人有些吃不消啊。
t_en: Such complexity can be overwhelming at times.

t_ch: 你先去忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 嗯，我正好去看看在烧的陶罐，下次再聊。
t_en: Sure, I'll go check on the dolia in the kiln. Catch you later.

t_ch: 你先去忙吧。
t_en: I'll leave you to your work.

t_ch: 嗯，我正好去看看在烧的陶罐，下次再聊。
t_en: Sure, I'll go check on the dolia in the kiln. Catch you later.

t_ch: 我想看下陶罐古玩。
t_en: I'd like to take a look at your dolia and artifacts.

t_ch: 嗯，我正好去看看在烧的陶罐，下次再聊。
t_en: Sure, I'll go check on the dolia in the kiln. Catch you later.

t_ch: 眼前的少女一动不动，双眼空洞，平等地无视周遭的一切，这份超然在喧闹的市集中显得有些格格不入……
t_en: The young woman before you sits frozen, her gaze vacant, seemingly detached from the bustling city around her, lost in a world of her own as if she is surreal...

t_ch: …她睡着了。
t_en: ...Or rather, she is sleeping.

t_ch: （大喊）有人偷书了！
t_en: (yelling) Someone's stealing books!

t_ch: （选择不打扰，转身离开）
t_en: (Leave quietly. Do not disturb her.)

t_ch: 我这是…重生了吗？前一刻，我还被绑于黎明机器之上，接受我亲生哥哥的判决。眼前的面孔逐渐清晰，只听男人一声令下，顷刻炼化了我体内的泰坦之血……
t_en: Did I experience... a rebirth? Just moments ago, I was bound to the Dawn Device, facing my brother's judgment. His visage grew fierce as he uttered the command, instantly eradicating the Titan blood from within me...

t_ch: …上一世，我是预言中翁法罗斯的救世主。我的降生给所有瑟希斯的信徒带来希望，树庭上下倾尽所有将我养育至预言中对抗黑潮的那一天。
t_en: ...In my previous life, I was the prophesied savior of Amphoreus. My birth instilled hope in every faithful disciple of Cerces, with the Grove sparing no expense in bringing me up for the day when I am prophesied to stand against the encroaching black tide.

t_ch: 可谁也没想到，我的真实身份竟是塞纳托斯秘传的嫡嫡嫡嫡嫡…嫡长女，注定为世间降下死亡与沉沦。我的哥哥奉瑟希斯之命饮下黄金血，亲手将我斩杀以免祸乱。
t_en: Little did they suspect that I was the great-great-great-great... granddaughter of Thanatos' clandestine lineage, fated to sow death and disorder in the realm of mortals. Following Cerces' will, my brother imbibed the golden blood and executed me personally to prevent further calamity.

t_ch: 这一世，我发誓要夺回属于自己的一切……
t_en: In this new life, I vow to reclaim what is rightfully mine...

t_ch: 等一下，这也太胡闹了吧！
t_en: Wait a sec. This is getting too whimsical!

t_ch: 重生之我是藏书所老板。
t_en: The time I was reincarnated as the librarium owner.

t_ch: 跳过广告。
t_en: Let's just skip the ads.

t_ch: 唔…刚刚…我应该是梦到了昨晚新写的情节嘿嘿。
t_en: Hmm... I guess... I must've been dreaming about the new story I wrote last night, hehehe.

t_ch: 这种晚上赶稿，白天看店补觉的生活，我早都习惯了，可脱口而出创作情节还是头一回，一定是这次的故事写得太有沉浸感了！
t_en: I've gotten used to the routine of running the store and sleeping by day, and writing at night, but coming up with a plot on the spot is a first for me. Maybe my tale is just too engrossing this time!

t_ch: 你！就当我刚是在胡言乱语，别和任何人提起，万一传到我哥耳朵里…我就完蛋了。
t_en: Hey, you! Forget what I just said and don't mention it to anyone. If my brother finds out... I'm toast.

t_ch: 有梦想不是挺好的吗！
t_en: But it's nice to have dreams, right?

t_ch: 哎…简单来说…他认为我们是瑟希斯的信徒，不应该……
t_en: Well... simply put... he thinks that we as followers of Cerces should not...

t_ch: 你哥要将你顷刻炼化？
t_en: Would he "instantly eradicate" you?

t_ch: 差不多这个感觉，简单来说…他认为我们是瑟希斯的信徒，不应该……
t_en: Well, something like that. Simply put... he thinks that we as followers of Cerces should not...

t_ch: 嘘…有客人来了，一会儿再聊。
t_en: Shh... we've got a customer. Let's catch up later.

t_ch: 瑟希斯信徒直营藏书所，想看点什么？
t_en: ...Cerces Followers' Librarium. How may I help you?

t_ch: 和我说说……
t_en: Tell me more about it...

t_ch: …周边无老哥出没，危机解除！所有事情记得替我保密呀。
t_en: ...Brother alarm lifted! Remember, what I say is just between us.

t_ch: 上次说到你们信仰瑟希斯……
t_en: You mentioned your faith in Cerces...

t_ch: 正是！我家世世代代都是瑟希斯的信徒，以传播学识为己任，这间藏书所也是祖传下来的一份虔诚。我老哥更是个卷王，早早博览群书，去生命花园当学者去了。
t_en: Yeah! My family has upheld faith in Cerces for generations, upholding the dissemination of knowledge as a sacred duty, and this librarium is a legacy from our ancestors. My brother is a super duper erudite scholar who read so much that he already earned his place as a Garden of Life scholar.

t_ch: 在我哥这个大学者眼里，我写的这些穿越、逆袭之类的爽文不够入流。要是被他知道了，肯定又要说这种冒险故事有悖于瑟希斯的理性精神，会触犯泰坦之怒。
t_en: My brother, the great scholar, thinks my made-up stories about underdogs and reincarnation are indecency. If he found them out, he'd probably say that they don't match Cerces' notion of rationality and could provoke the Titans.

t_ch: 我也只有隐忍，白天配合他之意看店，晚上偷偷写稿。
t_en: So, I have to juggle running the store during the day and writing novels in secret at night.

t_ch: 瑟希斯的信徒这么保守吗？
t_en: Are followers of Cerces so conservative?

t_ch: 不信仰瑟希斯不就好了？
t_en: Why not just give up your faith in Cerces?

t_ch: 欸，千万莫认为是瑟希斯的问题，我的喷涌的灵感也由博古通今的「裂分之枝」赐予。
t_en: Hey, don't blame Cerces. All my overflowing inspirations actually come from the Bough of Rift, which can see into the past and future.

t_ch: 要怪就怪我哥。泰奥弗斯，你这个老古板！瑟希斯在我笔下又活出了八百种精彩的神生，不比被你侍奉成个木头泰坦好？
t_en: Blame my brother. You old stiff, Theophis! Cerces has lived over <unbreak>800</unbreak> brilliant divine lives through my pen! Isn't that more exciting than being a dull Titan on your altar!?

t_ch: 你写的书哪里买？
t_en: How can I buy the books you've written?

t_ch: 嘘…别那么大声，隔墙有耳。
t_en: Shh... Keep your voice down. You never know who might be listening.

t_ch: 我都摆在后屋的小隔间里，免得被我哥看出破绽，要的话我去给你取两本。
t_en: I stash them away in a secret compartment in the back room to keep them safe from my brother's prying eyes. If you're interested, I can fetch you a couple.

t_ch: 说到这儿，我还有另一件头疼事——近期顾客们越来越爱看轻快的读物，情节爽快的故事、<unbreak>30</unbreak>秒速读的搞笑小石板都成了畅销品。
t_en: Speaking of that, there's a new challenge for me. Nowadays, customers prefer lighter fiction. Quick-paced plots, humorous tales, and mini comedy tablets featuring <unbreak>30</unbreak>-second stories are flying off the shelves.

t_ch: 供稿的作者越来越多，存书已经多到隔间快藏不下了，得想个法子……
t_en: With more authors joining in, I'm running out of space to store their books in my secret compartment. I need to find a solution...

t_ch: 为何不找你哥聊聊？
t_en: Why don't you talk about it with your brother?

t_ch: 虽说我老抱怨他思想陈旧，其实我并不求他做任何改变。
t_en: Even though I complain about his outdated views, deep down I don't really want him to change.

t_ch: 我们的父母很早前就在黑潮灾难中去世了。那时他不过六岁，却要负担起两个人的生活，几年后又张罗着家族藏书所的重建，想尽办法让我的精神也变得富足。
t_en: Our parents died in the black tide disaster long ago. He was just a six-year-old child back then, but he had to provide for both of us. As time passed, he worked on rebuilding our family librarium, while also doing the best he could to nurture me intellectually.

t_ch: 即使我已长大成人，他也习惯把我视作自己的责任……只是责任与过度保护的界线不太清晰就是啦嘿嘿。
t_en: Even though I'm an adult now, he still feels responsible for me... It's just that the line between care and overprotection is a bit blurry, hehe.

t_ch: 我不怪他，如果瞒着他能让他省份心的话，我宁愿自己辛苦点。
t_en: I don't blame him. I'd rather work harder on my own if keeping everything a secret makes life easier for him.

t_ch: 我没什么要打听的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 需要找什么再和我说，有些东西我不摆出来卖……
t_en: Let me know what you're looking for. There are books I don't put up for sale...

t_ch: 我没什么要打听的了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 需要找什么再和我说，有些东西我不摆出来卖……
t_en: Let me know what you're looking for. There are books I don't put up for sale...

t_ch: 我想买书。
t_en: I'd like to buy some books.

t_ch: 需要找什么再和我说，有些东西我不摆出来卖……
t_en: Let me know what you're looking for. There are books I don't put up for sale...

t_ch: …昨天到底怎么回事，你为什么没去？
t_en: ...What happened yesterday? Why didn't you show up?

t_ch: 再说一遍，我已经结婚了，所以请不要再试图给我介绍伴侣了。
t_en: Let me be straight: I'm already married. So, please stop trying to set me up with someone else.

t_ch: 那个地位低下的山之民，既没钱也没前途…他哪里配得上你，赶紧把他甩了吧！
t_en: That modest Mountain Dweller with no riches or prospects... Does he even measure up to you? Just ditch him!

t_ch: 请不要那么说他，朱诺婶婶。伏尔甘很好，既温柔又有耐心，而且非常踏实肯干……
t_en: Please stop badmouthing him, Aunt Juno. Vulcan is a gentle soul. He's caring, patient, and persistent...

t_ch: 那又怎么样，阿芙罗，你就听婶婶一句劝吧：像你这样的大美女，喜欢你的人排起队来都能绕集市一圈了，又何必吊死在一棵树上？
t_en: So what, Aphrea? Seriously, take my advice: With your beauty, why settle for him when you could have a queue of admirers eager to do anything just for your attention?

t_ch: 但我也喜欢他，这就够了。
t_en: But I'm in love with him. That's what matters.

t_ch: 嗐，你们年轻人想得就是简单…婚姻这东西哪能这么儿戏？你现在一时冲动，以后怕不是要后悔半辈子呢！
t_en: Tsk, you youngsters and your idealism... Marriage isn't a game. Rush into things now, and you'll regret it forever!

t_ch: 要我说啊，我给你介绍那位就很好。人家在原本的城邦曾经是个大贵族呢，就算来到了奥赫玛，血统和身份也不是一般人比得了的。
t_en: In my view, the man I introduced you to is remarkable. He was a prominent noble in his previous city, and even though he's in Okhema now, his heritage and status make him stand out.

t_ch: 不需要。
t_en: No.

t_ch: 诶呦，几个意思？人家还没嫌弃你呢，你怎么就…别不识好歹啊，我这可是为了你好！
t_en: Huh, what do you mean, "no"? It's your fortune that he might choose you, but why are you... Don't be ungrateful! I'm doing this for your sake!

t_ch: 为我好？呵，一般说这句话的人，真正想说的都是：「我其实是为了自己好，而你只是我的工具和踏板。」
t_en: For my sake? Hah! You know, when people say "for your sake," what they really mean is, "I'm doing this for myself, and you're just a means to an end."

t_ch: 你、你这孩子，怎么和长辈说话呢？！
t_en: W—Watch your tone when talking to your elder!

t_ch: 我记得表妹和我年纪相仿吧，你怎么不给她介绍？哦，我知道了，怕不是她的「品相」不够好，人家瞧不上。
t_en: What about my cousin, who's the same age as me? Why didn't you introduce her instead? Let me guess... it's because her appearance doesn't meet his "standards," right?

t_ch: 你…你的礼貌哪去了？！原本多乖巧的孩子啊，肯定是那个傻大个给你带坏了…我就知道那些山之民一个个都上不了台面……
t_en: Wh—Where are your manners!? You used to be so kind... but now? Must be the influence of that uncouth Mountain Dweller... I always knew those folks couldn't handle civilized society...

t_ch: 我可不这么觉得。来，照照镜子，好好瞧瞧你这幅气急败坏、却又死活不肯放下架子的嘴脸吧——看到了吗，我们家伏尔甘的模样可比这顺眼多了。
t_en: Well, I beg to differ. Take a good look in the mirror and see how worked-up you are over this. Notice that? My Vulcan looks leagues above that.
Tsk...

t_ch: 但朱诺婶婶，你也听我一句劝吧：别再试图干涉我的生活了。
t_en: I've got a piece of advice for you too, Aunt Juno. Stay. Out. Of. My. Business.

t_ch: 你也说过吧，喜欢我的人排起队来都能绕集市一圈啦。要是你再惹我伤心难过了，万一哪个疯狂的追求者因此对你做出一些不好的事情，那可就不是我能阻止的了，你说是吧？
t_en: Just like you said, I have a queue of admirers eager to do anything just for my attention. If you upset me too much, one of them might do something foolish to you. I can't keep an eye on those men all day, can I?

t_ch: …你、你竟敢威胁我…你…给我等着！
t_en: ...H—How dare you threaten me...? Just... just you wait!

t_ch: 名为朱诺的妇人脸色惨白，一边不断小声咒骂着，一边仓惶离去。
t_en: The face of the dame named Juno pales as she hurries off, muttering curses under her breath.

t_ch: 呼…看来总算可以清净一阵子了。今天真是个好天气啊~
t_en: Phew... Finally, some peace and quiet. Ah, what a lovely day today.

t_ch: 你好呀，这位客人——欢迎光临金织裁缝铺，请问有什么需要的吗？
t_en: Hey, there! Welcome to Goldweaver tailor! What can I help you with today?

t_ch: 你好呀，这位客人——欢迎光临金织裁缝铺，请问有什么需要的吗？
t_en: Hey, there! Welcome to Goldweaver tailor! What can I help you with today?

t_ch: 你似乎遇到了什么麻烦。
t_en: You seem to be in a bit of trouble.

t_ch: 唔…如你所见，我不久前缔结了姻缘，但他们都觉得我的丈夫配不上我。
t_en: Well, you see, I recently got married, but everyone is convinced that I'm too good for my husband.

t_ch: 就因为伏尔甘是个外来的山之民…他们甚至问我是不是受到了胁迫才嫁给他，哈哈。
t_en: All because Vulcan is an outlander, a Mountain Dweller... They even asked me if I was forced into marrying him, heh.

t_ch: 山之民怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong with Mountain Dwellers?

t_ch: 是啊，山之民怎么了！他只是平时看上去老实木讷，但如果认真交流的话，你就能发现他人其实非常好的！
t_en: Beats me! I mean, what's wrong with Mountain Dwellers!? Sure, he might seem a little dull, but once you get to know him, you'll see his heart of gold!

t_ch: 哼，说什么「不堪入目的傻大个」…呸呸呸，要是真贪图个好看的皮囊，我自己照镜子不好吗？明明美丽的灵魂才是最重要的！
t_en: Tsk, all this chatter about being "unattractive and dim-witted"... and blah blah... Heh, if I wanted eye candy, I'd just gaze at myself in the mirror. A beautiful soul is what truly counts!

t_ch: 你们的感情究竟怎么样？
t_en: How are things really going between you two?

t_ch: 当然是特、别、美、满、啦！你知道吗，我的追求者虽然很多，但他们追求我，更像是想要追求「美」的事物，并将其据为己有。
t_en: It's PURE BLISS! You know, while I've had plenty of admirers, they're more interested in possessing something beautiful for themselves.

t_ch: 只有伏尔甘，他看到的始终是真正的那部分「我」——他总是乐意倾听我的想法、尊重我的意愿、理解我的追求，简直就是「灵魂伴侣」！
t_en: But only Vulcan sees the real me. He always hears me out, values my thoughts, and supports my ambitions. He's simply my soul mate!

t_ch: 你很有主见。
t_en: You've definitely got a mind of your own.

t_ch: 谢谢夸奖~其实这都多亏了阿格莱雅阁下的教导，她虽然看上去有点…稍稍有点难以接近，但实际上是一位温柔的人。
t_en: Thanks for saying that! It's all thanks to the wisdom of Lady Aglaea. She might come off as... intimidating, but deep down, she has a gentle soul.

t_ch: 她让我学会了很多，尤其是…「美」。这种事物绝非仅限于眼之所见，而是需要用心去感受。
t_en: She's taught me a lot, especially about... beauty. It's not just about pleasing the eye, but about nourishing the soul.

t_ch: 下次有机会再讲吧。
t_en: Let us talk next time.

t_ch: 那么再见~有需要的话，欢迎随时光临哦。
t_en: Catch you later! Drop by anytime if you need a hand with anything.

t_ch: 店里都有些什么？
t_en: What do you have in stock?

t_ch: 裁缝铺嘛，当然是售卖布料、制定成衣喽。既然来了，客人不妨看看有没有喜欢的布料？
t_en: Since we're a tailor's shop, you can expect to find fabrics and ready-made garments. Take a look around and see if anything catches your eye!

t_ch: 白麻布。
t_en: White linen, please.

t_ch: 这个白麻布是整个奥赫玛应用最广泛的布料。走在街上你能看见的十个人里，有九个都穿它制成的衣服呢。
t_en: White linen is a staple fabric in Okhema. It's everywhere — chances are, nine out of ten people you see are wearing clothes made from it.

t_ch: 瞧瞧，我们店里织布用的白麻都是生命花园培育的新品种，既柔软又透气，垂感还特别好。来，摸摸看——是不是像蝴蝶一样轻盈！
t_en: Check this out! The white linen we carry is a new variety grown in the Garden of Life. It's soft, breathable, and drapes like a dream. Here, feel it! It's light as a butterfly, isn't it?

t_ch: 客人不考虑制定一套本地的服饰吗？你的皮肤很白，头发和眼睛的颜色也很漂亮，穿起来一定非常有气质。
t_en: Have you thought about ordering a set of our local attire? Your fair skin, lovely hair, and beautiful eye color would make you look stunning in one.

t_ch: 紫纱料。
t_en: Purple silk, please.

t_ch: 这种紫纱最早起源于自东方的皮利亚城邦，那里的农户会专门喂养一种能吐丝的柞虫。他们将虫丝纺织成纱料，然后用螺旋贝制成的特殊粉末将它们染成紫色。
t_en: This purple silk originally came from the city-state of Pyria in the east. Farmers there specialized in raising a unique worm that produced silk. They spun the strands into silk and dyed it purple using a special powder from spiral shells.

t_ch: 不过那个城邦早就已经消失啦，如今市面上能见到的紫纱都是人工合成的，染剂也都换成了蓝菘石和赤叶草。
t_en: But Pyria has long been a thing of the past. Today's purple silk is synthetic, and the dye was changed to blue brassica stones and redleaf grass.

t_ch: 由于制作工艺复杂，这种纱料的价格始终比较昂贵。人们一般会用它制作礼服，在祭典之类的场合穿戴。
t_en: Due to its intricate method of production, this silk tends to be on the pricier side. It's often used for crafting formal wear at festivals and grand events.

t_ch: 至于平时嘛…哈哈，要是你有那种看上去就特别严肃、总喜欢板着脸的朋友，倒是可以让对方试试。
t_en: For everyday wear... Well, if you know someone who's always serious and stern, maybe you could recommend it to them.

t_ch: 金丝绸。
t_en: Golden silk, please.

t_ch: 哇，客人可真有眼光——你现在看的这匹丝绸由阿格莱雅大人亲手纺织，是本店的镇店之宝哦。
t_en: You've got a keen eye! This silk you're looking at is spun by Lady Aglaea herself! It's a gem of this humble shop!

t_ch: 说到这个，就不得不给客人你讲个故事啦：据说曾有位逐火的黄金裔只身狩猎神祇，战斗时泰坦的金血染在她的手上。后来她回到家乡，发现自己纺出的丝线都是华丽的金丝。
t_en: Let me share this tale with you! Legend has it that a Flame-Chase Chrysos Heir once battled the gods alone, and her hands were stained with the golden ichor of the Titans. When she returned home, she discovered that all the silk she spun turned a dazzling golden hue.

t_ch: 当然啦，实际上这种金色来源于一种稀有矿石制成的染剂。但这并不影响金丝绸的价格非常昂贵，只有很少数的人才用得起哦。
t_en: In fact, its golden color comes from a dye made from a rare ore. But even so, the golden silk is quite pricey and only within reach for a select few.

t_ch: 没什么想要的。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 没关系~有需要的话，欢迎随时光临哦。
t_en: No worries at all! Feel free to drop in whenever you need.

t_ch: 不是，我就不明白了，躺着吃饭已经够离谱了，怎么同一道菜里的不同配料还得按顺序吃啊？
t_en: Hey, I just don't get it. It's odd enough to eat lying down, and now I have to consume the ingredients in a specific order in a single dish!?

t_ch: 请稍安勿躁，您点的这道「古典拼盘」是奥赫玛的传统美食，历史上人们在食用这道菜时，为了能充分享受其美味，都会按照……
t_en: Please calm down. This "Classical Platter" you ordered is a traditional Okhema dish. Historically, to fully appreciate its flavors, people used to follow...

t_ch: 停停停！我是来吃饭的，不是来上历史课的！你个开餐厅的就好好做饭不行吗？这么喜欢教育人，怎么不去云石天宫外面讲课啊？
t_en: Wait a sec! I came here to eat, not for a history lesson! This is a restaurant — Can you just focus on serving food!? If you're all about giving lectures, why not do that outside the Marmoreal Palace?

t_ch: …抱歉给您添麻烦了，但美食为人们带来的从不只是味蕾的刺激，还有精神上的慰藉与享受。所以如果不去了解其所在的文化背景，就……
t_en: ...I apologize for any inconvenience, but food isn't just about the chemical excitations on your tongue. It's also about finding spiritual comfort. Without understanding the historical and cultural significance, it's...

t_ch: 够了够了！你是不是脑子有病啊？都说了我对这些玩意没兴趣！等等——
t_en: Stop it! I've had enough! Are you nuts or what? I already told you I'm not interested! Oh, I see...

t_ch: 好啊，我知道了，你怕不是因为我是个外邦人就故意找茬吧？那我不吃了还不行吗！退钱！
t_en: Oh, I get it now. You're trying to mess with an outlander like me, aren't you? Fine, I won't be eating here, I want my money back!

t_ch: 我并无此意，如果您实在不愿意，我也不会勉强……
t_en: I meant no offense, dear customer. If you really don't want to learn about history, I won't push any further...

t_ch: 桌上的拼盘被愤怒的食客挥手砸在地上，发出「啪」的一声脆响——
t_en: In a fit of rage, the customer hurls his hand, sending the plate on the table crashing to the floor with a loud pop.

t_ch: 我就知道你们这些本地人根本瞧不上外邦的流民！假惺惺地做给谁看呢？什么奥赫玛文化传统，我看被历史淘汰的糟粕还差不多！
t_en: I knew you locals wouldn't treat us outlanders with respect! Who are you trying to impress? Talking about Okhema's culture and history? Hah, sounds more like trash destined to be forgotten by time!

t_ch: 嘴上说什么不勉强，实际上我们这些可怜人被勉强得还少吗？我告诉你，就你做的这些破玩意，大地兽都不吃！我……
t_en: You say you won't push us, but how many times were we outlanders pushed into things we didn't want? You know what? Even a dromas wouldn't lick the mess you serve! I...

t_ch: 「啪——」的又一声脆响，却是那位暴怒的食客被丢出门外。你很难想象餐厅老板那并不过分健硕的身躯是如何爆发出如此强大的力量的。
t_en: Then you hear another loud "pop" as the enraged customer is forcefully thrown out through the door. You can hardly imagine how the seemingly feeble restaurant owner unleashes such a ferocious display of strength.

t_ch: 唯有美食与文化不可亵渎…请不要再出现在这家店了，不然后果自负……
t_en: Food and culture must be respected... Please don't ever show up in my place again, or you'll do it at your own risk...

t_ch: 嗯？有新客人啊——欢迎光临，请问您想来点什么？
t_en: Hmm? Oh, a new customer. Welcome! What can I get for you today?

t_ch: 你这规矩好像还不少。
t_en: Seems like you have some strict rules here.

t_ch: 让您见笑了，规矩谈不上，这家餐厅只有一条底线：绝对不可以糟蹋食物。
t_en: I'm sorry for any misunderstanding. We don't have rules, just one principle: Never disrespect food.

t_ch: 除此之外，我很乐意尽己所能，满足顾客们对于食物的一切需求。
t_en: For those who appreciate food, I'll do my best to serve top-notch dishes.

t_ch: 我不会也被扔出去吧？
t_en: You won't throw me out too, will you?

t_ch: 很抱歉让您看到我如此失礼的模样，但请放心……
t_en: I'm sorry for showing such an unsightly scene to you, but please rest assured...

t_ch: 相信您怎么都不会是那种肆意糟蹋食物的人，对吧？
t_en: I trust you wouldn't deliberately ruin a good dish, right?

t_ch: 刚才那家伙可真没礼貌！
t_en: That guy was terribly rude!

t_ch: 让您见笑了，不过那样的家伙确实没有资格在这里用餐——
t_en: I'm sorry for the disturbance, but those who don't value food have no place dining here...

t_ch: 身为一名厨师，我非常乐意虚心听取食客们的意见，但前提是绝对不可以糟蹋食物。
t_en: As a chef, I really appreciate hearing what customers think, as long as they show respect for food.

t_ch: 好啦，方才的小插曲就让它过去吧——您想来点什么呢？本地的特色美食，还是那些早已失传的外邦菜肴？
t_en: Well, let's move past that incident. What would you like to eat? Local specialties? Or perhaps something more outlandish that has been lost to time?

t_ch: 您想来点什么呢？本地的特色美食，还是那些早已失传的外邦菜肴？
t_en: What would you like to eat? Local specialties? Or perhaps something more outlandish that has been lost to time?

t_ch: 躺着吃饭是怎么回事？
t_en: What's the deal with eating while lying down?

t_ch: 这个嘛，「躺食」其实是奥赫玛的一种传统习俗。在遥远的黄金世，人们曾认为侧躺在椅子上进食，能够更好地与大地泰坦产生共鸣。
t_en: Well, "reclining dining" is actually an old tradition here in Okhema. Back in the ancient Era Chrysea, people believed that reclining on chairs while dining helped them resonate with the Earth Titan.

t_ch: 对他们而言，这样的用餐方式不仅有益身体健康——主要是有助于消化——还能表达对于吉奥里亚的崇敬与感激。
t_en: For them, this unique way of eating wasn't just about the health benefits like aiding digestion. It was a gesture of respect and gratitude towards Georios.

t_ch: 至于为什么要推广它…在我看来，若是脱离了文化与传统，再美味的菜肴也都是无根之水，无法长久地流传。
t_en: As for why I'm promoting this style... In my opinion, a dish loses its root and can no longer be passed down when it's severed from its cultural roots.

t_ch: 在顺应时代需求的基础上，对传统美食文化做出改良与推广——这就是我的人生目标。
t_en: My goal is to preserve and modernize traditional food culture while keeping pace with the new world.

t_ch: 不过…当然啦，我也知道在这种世道下，人们往往都自顾不暇，又怎么愿意花费太多心思去了解食物的历史与文化呢。这也是没办法的事嘛，哈哈……
t_en: But I understand... Life's busy these days, and not everyone has time to dive into food history and heritage. Guess that's just the way things are, hah...

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 哈哈，没关系，欢迎下次光临。
t_en: No worries! Looking forward to your next visit.

t_ch: 有什么本地特色？
t_en: What local specialties do you have on the menu?

t_ch: 传说黄金世代的奥赫玛在饮食方面极为精细讲究，甚至可谓铺张奢靡。后来随着黑潮的不断侵蚀，资源愈发匮乏，人们的饮食习惯也渐渐变得朴素起来。
t_en: Legend has it that during the Era Chrysea, Okhema's cuisine was meticulous and even extravagant. But as resources dwindled due to the black tide's unceasing invasion, simplicity took over in terms of culinary habits.

t_ch: 本店的宗旨就是用最简单的食材与手法，复刻出历史上那些美味佳肴。在此为您推荐本店的招牌菜——「古典拼盘」。
t_en: I'm trying to bring back those lavish historical dishes using the simplest ingredients and techniques. So, I highly recommend our signature dish, the "Classical Platter."

t_ch: 这道菜原本的做法极为复杂，至少需要几十道工序。我在保留其主要风味的基础上极大简化了做法，但进餐顺序仍会影响口感…介意我稍稍为您讲解一下吗？
t_en: Originally, this dish was a complex masterpiece with several dozen steps. I've greatly simplified it while retaining its main flavors, but the order you taste the ingredients in is still crucial... Do you mind if I delve into it?

t_ch: 好啊，那就给我讲讲呗。
t_en: Not at all. I'm all ears.

t_ch: 一刻钟后，一个拼盘被端到你面前。盘中的菜肴分为内外两层：外层半圈是切好的蛋卷、半圈是齐整的肉排；内圈是去皮的苹果块，上面淋着琥珀色的粘稠酱汁。
t_en: A quint later, a platter is served before you. The dish is presented in two layers: Egg rolls and flank steak bisect the outer edge in half, while peeled apple chunks doused in a rich amber sauce sit in the center.

t_ch: 首先是前菜——请尝尝您右手边的黄金蛋卷，它由禽蛋配上水犀乳、异果汁小火烧制而成，非常适合开胃。
t_en: Now, please enjoy the golden egg rolls on your right first — a mix of poultry egg rolls, aquarhino milk, and a special fruit sauce boiled over low heat, a perfect starter.

t_ch: 滑而不腻的蛋卷在你口中绽放，伴随着鲜乳的微甘与水果的清香。你的胃发出满足的喟叹，但与此同时，你觉得自己更饿了。
t_en: The smooth egg roll delights your palate with the sweetness of the milk and the nectarous aroma of the fruit. Your hunger is sated, yet your appetite craves more.

t_ch: 接下来是主菜——请尝尝左边的肉排，它选用新鲜的岩羊肋排肉，配上秘制酱料炭烤而成，无论在营养还是口感层面都能满足大部分人群。
t_en: Next, please savor the main course — the juicy rocklamb rib meat on the left, charcoal-grilled in my secret sauce, a balance of nutrition and flavor.

t_ch: 羊排肉肥瘦相间，鲜嫩无比，却没有丝毫膻气。你将自己埋进盘子里，吃相堪比一只饿了三天的浣熊。
t_en: The lamb steak is tender, juicy, and incredibly flavorful without the gamey flavor. You devour it eagerly like a hungry raccoon after three days without food.

t_ch: 最后是甜点——整个奥赫玛最新鲜的苹果，配上我改良过的肉桂蜂蜜酱，刚好适合为这一餐做个完美的收尾。
t_en: Finally, we've got dessert — the freshest apples in Okhema paired with my upgraded cinnamon honey sauce, a delightful end to your meal.

t_ch: 你的口腔被肉桂强势的辛香所席卷，肉排残余的油脂被一扫而空；但蜂蜜的香甜与苹果的清甜转瞬又让这味道变得柔和起来，成为一种若有似无的余韵。
t_en: The spiciness of cinnamon gives a sharp kick, cleansing any lingering greasiness from the lamb. But the pleasant combination of honey and apples instantly softens the experience, leaving a lingering aftertaste.

t_ch: 你久久沉醉于这美妙口感带来的享受中，难以自拔。
t_en: Engrossed in the wonderful flavors, you relish each and every bite.

t_ch: 看来您对这道菜的评价还算不错，我的荣幸。承蒙惠顾，<unbreak>1000</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: Seems like you're really enjoying this dish. I'm glad you like it. Thank you for your patronage. It's <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> Balance Coins.

t_ch: 免了，我就随便尝一口。
t_en: It's okay, I'll just eat it in my own order.

t_ch: 好的，那么您请自便。祝您有个好胃口。
t_en: No problem. Enjoy your meal. Good appetite.

t_ch: 一刻钟后，一个拼盘被端到你面前。盘中的菜肴分为内外两层：外层半圈是切好的蛋卷、半圈是齐整的肉排；内圈是去皮的苹果块，上面淋着琥珀色的粘稠酱汁。
t_en: A quint later, a platter is served before you. The dish is presented in two layers: Egg rolls and flank steak bisect the outer edge in half, while peeled apple chunks doused in a rich amber sauce sit in the center.

t_ch: 你尝尝这个、品品那个，囫囵地将拼盘里的菜肴拌在一起吃下——
t_en: Trying the ingredients in a haphazard order, you eventually mix everything on the platter and consume it all...

t_ch: 好像挺好吃的，又好像有点怪…总之你也说不出个所以然来。
t_en: The taste is pleasant but slightly weird... although you can't quite figure out why.

t_ch: …就这？
t_en: ...That's it?

t_ch: 我方才对您说过，这道菜的食用顺序会影响口感。那么承蒙惠顾，<unbreak>1000</unbreak>利衡币。
t_en: As I said, the order in which you savor the ingredients can really change the taste. Thank you for your patronage. It's <unbreak>1,000</unbreak> Balance Coins.

t_ch: 算了，我突然不想吃了。
t_en: I suddenly lost my appetite.

t_ch: 哈哈，没关系，欢迎下次光临。
t_en: No worries! Looking forward to your next visit.

t_ch: 有什么外邦美食？
t_en: What foreign dishes do you have on the menu?

t_ch: 由于黑潮的缘故，这些年陆续有不少外邦人移居奥赫玛。我有幸从他们那得到了一些食谱，并结合其历史文化进行了复刻与适当的改良。这些是我比较满意的作品——
t_en: Over the years, many outlanders have made their home in Okhema due to the black tide. I've been fortunate enough to get some of their recipes and put my own spin on them based on the relevant history and cultures. Here are a couple of my favorites...

t_ch: 粗制葡萄秘酿。
t_en: Crude Grape Brew.

t_ch: 很久以前，人们在前往奥赫玛的途中，面临的最大困难并非食物短缺、黑潮泛滥，而是洁净水源的匮乏。这也是「粗制葡萄秘酿」的由来。
t_en: In the past, the biggest challenge for travelers heading to Okhema was finding clean water, even worse than lacking food or the threat of the black tide. That's how the Crude Grape Brew came to be.

t_ch: 比起其它秘酿的繁琐制作流程，它只需要一个极为简单的仪式，就能通过法吉娜的赐福快速得到救命的良药——不但洁净无害，甚至含有人体必需的营养成分……
t_en: Unlike other brews with elaborate manufacturing processes, this one's a life-saving medicine that can quickly invoke Phagousa's blessing with an extremely simple ritual. It's safe, clean, and even packs essential nutrients...

t_ch: 你好奇地尝了一口所谓的粗制葡萄秘酿——呸呸呸！这分明是草席子泡水的味道！！！
t_en: You take a sip of the crude grape brew... EWW! It tastes like a soggy straw mat!!!

t_ch: 你砸吧砸吧嘴，半晌才从这股怪味中回过神来。而此时你才后知后觉地品尝到其中极为浅淡的葡萄味。
t_en: It hits your taste buds hard, leaving you stunned by the unexpected flavor. Only later do you catch a very faint hint of grape in the aftertaste.

t_ch: 诶呀，我还没说完呢…这种秘酿既干净又营养，唯一的问题就是口感粗糙、味道非常…独特。不过由于价格便宜又容易制作，迄今为止它仍旧有着不小的市场。
t_en: Hey, I'm not done explaining yet... While it's clean and nutritious, its taste is coarse and... well, unique. Still, it's a hit here because it's cheap and easy to make.

t_ch: 蜜果羹。
t_en: Fig stew.

t_ch: 这是麦迪特瑞恩文化中一种著名的甜汤，最具特色的就是汤里由蜜果制成的果冻。
t_en: This a famous sweet soup from the Mediterrana culture and is famous for its unique feature: A jelly made from figs in the soup.

t_ch: 但在那座城邦覆灭之后，麦迪特瑞恩蜜果也跟着灭绝了。即便后来生命花园又重新培育出了那种果子的改良版，价格上却不怎么亲民。
t_en: Sadly, with the fall of the city, Mediterranan figs became extinct. Although the Garden of Life has bred a new version, it's not exactly budget-friendly.

t_ch: 所以我用奥赫玛本地的奶浆果作为代替品——就结果来看，这个尝试还是挺成功的，哈哈。
t_en: Therefore, I used our milkberries in Okhema instead, and it was a pretty successful attempt, if I do say so myself!

t_ch: 甜汤的用料非常丰富，各种豆子、葡萄干、芋头块，还有最重要的——奶浆果冻。
t_en: This sweet stew is a mix of various ingredients like beans, raisins, yam chunks, and the star of the show: Milkberry jelly.

t_ch: 汤的口感就像它的用料一样，丰富且具有层次感：豆类的软糯、果干的醇甜、芋头的柔韧，以及果冻的清新爽滑。
t_en: The texture of the stew is as intricate as its components: The tenderness of the beans, the gentle sweetness of the raisins, the creamy yam, and the refreshing smoothness of the jelly.

t_ch: 你狠狠吃了个爽。
t_en: You dig in and enjoy it to your heart's content.

t_ch: 没什么想吃的。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 哈哈，没关系，欢迎下次光临。
t_en: No worries! Looking forward to your next visit.

t_ch: 看看外带的快餐。
t_en: Take a look at the takeout.

t_ch: 你站在名为「法吉娜盲盒」的饮料前。据说欢宴之神要求「宴会上的秘酿不允许标注内容」。从平淡如水到一口晕倒都有可能，以增添宴会的混乱与趣味性。
t_en: You stand before a beverage known as "Phagousa Blind Box." It's said that the God of Banquets demands that the "ambrosia at the feasts not have their contents labeled." From a drink as mild as water to one that knocks you out cold with a single sip, it's all about adding chaos and fun to the party.

t_ch: 至少从外观上看，你无法察觉到它与一杯水之间有任何区别——
t_en: From the appearance, at least, you can't tell one drink from another —

t_ch: 一口吨了。
t_en: Down it in one gulp.

t_ch: 眼前一黑。
t_en: Everything goes black.

t_ch: 咕嘟…咕嘟…酸麻的味道自舌尖蔓延，脑袋也开始变得昏沉。
t_en: Bloop bloop... A sour, tingling sensation spreads from your tongue, and your head grows heavy.

t_ch: 坏了，饮下女口止匕烈性的一木不，你的目艮前出王见了重重重重影，亻尔有点看不氵青这都是亻十么宇宇宇字宇了。
t_en: Uh-oh, after drin king the potent brew, everyt hing around you starts to bbbblur, and y ou see mmmmultiple versions of the same scene. You can't quite make s ense of iiiit all.

t_ch: 亻尔摇了摇头，努力睁大眼睛，向前伸出手手手——
t_en: You shaaake your head, strug gling to open your eyes wide. Your haaaands reach out ahead, but—

t_ch: 土不了，亻尔感至刂自己的手止不亻主土地在亶页扌斗，这木不秘酉良的后劲非常弓虽力，你已经彳切底认不氵青这些歹衣刂开的字了，救求攵……
t_en: Wait, your haaaands moooove on their own, as if not en tirely under your control. The am bro sia's aftereffects are ki cking in hard, leaving you disori ented. The words before you are nothing more than a jum ble, their mea ning slipping through your fin gers...

t_ch: 衣匠能把它们缝起来吗？
t_en: Can a Garmentmaker stitch them back together?

t_ch: 衣…匠…？
t_en: Garmentmaker...

t_ch: 「米的妮丝，我遭您擅场红补，蛮铃把这些乱恰凹的嗯字缝起哎吧！」
t_en: "Melody, I know you're greet at twitching, good you grease phoenix these rumbled worlds?"

t_ch: ……口齿不氵青。
t_en: ...Your words are slurrrring.

t_ch: 衣匠答应了你的要求，数道几乎快不可察的光线在眼前掠过，乱码的文字重新被缝到了一起。
t_en: The Garmentmaker agrees to help you, and a faint shimmer passes before your eyes as the chaotic text is sewn back together.

t_ch: 我猜您想说「美丽的女士，我知道您擅长缝补，麻烦您把这些乱七八糟的文字缝起来吧！」——而我已经做到了。
t_en: I think you meant to say: "My lady, I know you're great at stitching, could you please fix these scrambled words?" — and I've done just that.

t_ch: 毕竟，您眼中的世界倘若如此散乱，实在不符合美观。我已尽力效劳。
t_en: After all, if the world you see is in such disarray, it would hardly be beautiful. I've done my best to assist.

t_ch: 昏光庭院，我要看眼科…
t_en: Twilight Courtyard? I need to see an eye doctor...

t_ch: 昏…光…庭…院…？
t_en: Twilight... Courtyard...

t_ch: 研表究明，文字的乱码并不定一能影阅响读，比如当灰宝你读这完句话后，才发这现里的字全是都乱的。
t_en: Studies show that text garbled not does always affect comprehension. For example, Grayie, after this sentence reading, you'll realize that all the words here are jumbled.

t_ch: 人的大脑在理解一些事情时，会根据已经捕捉的信息进行理解，这是正常现象。安心闭上眼睛，我为你施一些加天空的赐福，过一小会，就起好来啦！
t_en: The human brain tends to fill in the gaps when processing information, based on what information we already have — it's a natural phenomenon. Close your eyes and relax, I'll grant you a blessing of Sky, and soon, you'll be feeling better!

t_ch: 你不确定刚才眼前的景象和对话的人是否是*真实*的，或者那只是欢宴之神赐予的幻觉。好在文字的完型破坏以及重影皆已消失，你眼中的世界也重归秩序。
t_en: You're unsure whether the strange scene before you and the person you conversed with was real or just a hallucination from the God of Banquets. Fortunately, the destruction of language and blurry visuals have vanished, and the world around you has returned to its natural order.

t_ch: 嗝～
t_en: ...Burp~

t_ch: 一口吨了。
t_en: Down it in one gulp.

t_ch: 眼前一黑。
t_en: Everything goes black.

t_ch: 这一杯是跳跳糖一样的口感，一口下去，嘴巴里炸开了无数个微酸的气泡…嗷呜？你好像听到了自己嗓子眼里发出了小奇美拉的叫声……
t_en: This drink tastes like popping candy, bursting with countless tangy bubbles the moment it hits your mouth... Awoo? You think you hear a little chimera-like sound coming from your throat...

t_ch: 「砰！」的一声，你变成了一只…小奇美拉？
t_en: "Bang!" With a sudden jolt, you've transformed into... a little chimera?

t_ch: 嗷呜嗷呜？
t_en: Awoo? Awoo?

t_ch: 咦？刚才还看到灰宝在这里的，结果只是一只小奇美拉？
t_en: Hmm? I just saw Grayie standing here, but now, it's just a little chimera?

t_ch: 嗷呜！
t_en: Awooo!

t_ch: 嗷呜？
t_en: Awooo?

t_ch: 嗷呜嗷呜…
t_en: Awoo... Awoo...

t_ch: 能听懂奇美拉对话的风堇，不知能否听懂「风堇，是我！」，但你只是发出「嗷呜」。
t_en: Can Hyacine, who understands chimera talk, understand "Hyacine, it's me!"? But all you can manage is an "Awoo."

t_ch: …抱歉，看来你认识我呢，说起来，灰宝去哪里了？
t_en: ...Sorry, looks like you recognize me. Speaking of which, where did Grayie go?

t_ch: 太好了，风堇接受过{RUBY_B#理性之泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}祝福，能听懂你嗷呜了啥。
t_en: Great! Hyacine, having received the blessing of {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}, can now understand your woofs.

t_ch: （我就在这里）
t_en: (I'm right here.)

t_ch: 嗷呜嗷呜嗷！
t_en: Awoo awoo awoo!

t_ch: 哦。原来你也叫灰宝吗？
t_en: Oh. So you're also called Grayie?

t_ch: （{F#她}{M#他}刚才走了）
t_en: ({F#She}{M#He} just left.)

t_ch: 嗷，呜呜嗷。
t_en: Awoo, awoo awoo.

t_ch: 谢谢你哦。
t_en: Thank you.

t_ch: （救命啊，我变成奇美拉了）
t_en: (Help, I've turned into a chimera!)

t_ch: 嗷呜呜，嗷嗷嗷呜。
t_en: ...Awoo! Ah... ah awoo.

t_ch: …什么意思，难道说，你是……
t_en: ...What do you mean? Could it be... You're...

t_ch: …喝了「法吉娜盲盒」的小奇美拉？怪不得满嘴胡话。
t_en: ...A little chimera who drank some "Phagousa Blind Box"? No wonder you're babbling nonsense.

t_ch: 那我就先离开了。对了，懂事的小奇美拉可千万不要碰「法吉娜盲盒」哟。
t_en: Well, I'll leave you to it. By the way, a wise little chimera should definitely not drink the "Phagousa Blind Box."

t_ch: 「先入为主」对于新信息的理解影响显著…你很难在短时间内向别人解释清楚你就是{NICKNAME}…当然这没关系，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的神威很快就过效了。
t_en: "First impressions" can significantly influence how new information is understood... It's hard to convince others in a short time that you're {NICKNAME}... but that's fine, the power of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} will soon wear off.

t_ch: 又是「砰！」的一声，你重新变回了人样。
t_en: Another "bang!" and you return to your human form.

t_ch: 幸好，你的变形记只维持了几句嗷呜的时间。究竟是{NICKNAME}梦了奇美拉，还是奇美拉梦了{NICKNAME}，就不得而知了。
t_en: Fortunately, your transformation only lasted for a few "awoos." Whether {NICKNAME} dreamed of a chimera or the chimera dreamed of {NICKNAME}... it's hard to say.

t_ch: 一口吨了。
t_en: Down it in one gulp.

t_ch: 你屏住呼吸，这次「法吉娜盲盒」生效的感觉似乎慢了一些……
t_en: You hold your breath. The effects of the "Phagousa Blind Box" seem to kick in slower this time...

t_ch: 咂摸一番滋味…
t_en: Well, the flavor...

t_ch: 真是美味极啦…虽然这种廉价的评价很容易脱口而出，但事实上它…平淡如水。
t_en: Delicious indeed... Though compliments can be cheap like that, the truth is... it tastes as plain as water.

t_ch: 在它生效前做好准备…
t_en: Get ready before it takes effect...

t_ch: 可是你到底能做什么准备呢？提前通知昏光庭院的人准备好医疗措施、或者提前通知{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}信仰的朋友来帮忙……
t_en: But what can you do to get ready? Should you inform the people in Twilight Courtyard to get ready with medical measures, or perhaps notify a friend who follows {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} for help...

t_ch: 说到底，它不一定意味着你会遭遇不测，所以…倒也不必如此消极。
t_en: After all, it doesn't necessarily mean something bad is going to happen, so... maybe you need not be so pessimistic.

t_ch: 你静静地站在原地，像是在期待着什么——它也许真的什么效果也没有，但是它也许是有些不太明显的效果等待着你深度挖掘？
t_en: You stand still, almost waiting for something — perhaps it will have no effect at all, or perhaps there's a subtle effect waiting for you to discover?

t_ch: 所以它就是一杯普通的水。
t_en: So, it's just a cup of water.

t_ch: 感谢您使用我们的服务！请为我们的服务打分：
t_en: Thank you for using our service! Please rate our service:
[★★★★★] Very satisfied
[★★★★] Satisfied
[★★★] Neither satisfied nor dissatisfied

t_ch: [★★] 不满意
t_en: [★★] Unsatisfied

t_ch: [★] 非常不满意
t_en: [★] Very unsatisfied

t_ch: 很多构成我们日常生活的点点滴滴都没有机会被「评分」，就比如你平时喝下的一杯普普通通的水，也许很难用「评分」来衡量它的重要性。
t_en: Many aspects of our daily lives rarely get a chance to be "rated," like the ordinary glass of water you drink. It's hard to measure the importance of something so simple with a "rating."

t_ch: 别因为评分限制了你对美好的想象力！
t_en: But don't let a rating limit your imagination of what could be wonderful!

t_ch: 它一定存在着什么隐藏效果！
t_en: There must be some hidden effect here!

t_ch: 所以它是什么呢？
t_en: So, what is it?

t_ch: 这周内必然会一笔小财进账。
t_en: This week will bring a small windfall.

t_ch: 数量不多，买点小零食吧。
t_en: It won't be much, but maybe enough for some snacks.

t_ch: 必然能收获一段独处的时间。
t_en: It's certain to get a moment of solitude.

t_ch: 拿来干什么呢？打游戏，看视频，还是发呆？
t_en: What to do with that time? Play games, watch videos, or just zone out?

t_ch: 必然见到久未见面的老朋友。
t_en: Meeting with an old friend one hasn't seen in a while is on the table.

t_ch: 要是见到了，你可要想起来哦。
t_en: When you meet them, don't forget to remember this moment.

t_ch: 即便这是一杯没有任何附加效果的秘酿，也有一个祝福「始终保持着期待」送给你。
t_en: Even if this is just an ordinary ambrosia with no extra effects, there's still a blessing of "always keep hope alive" for you.

t_ch: 一点小心意，不足挂齿。
t_en: It's nothing. Don't mention it.

t_ch: 一口吨了。
t_en: Down it in one gulp.

t_ch: …这次，好像没有眼前一黑。
t_en: This time, it seems like your vision doesn't go black.

t_ch: 咕咚咕咚…秘酿全部下肚，你无法用任何词汇描述它的口感，因为它令你的大脑一片空白，眼前也出现了渐入的白……
t_en: Bloop bloop... the ambrosia goes down in one go, and you can't describe its taste with any words, because it leaves your mind completely blank, and your vision starts to fade into a whit-

t_ch: *非静止画面*
t_en: *This is not a static image*

t_ch: 耳边传来心电图机「哔哔」的声音，也许是有人在正在观察你的每个心跳周期。
t_en: You hear the "beep beep" sound of an electrocardiogram in your ears. Maybe someone is observing every heartbeat cycle.

t_ch: 根据你的经验，这样的声音是能够转化成波形图的，你开始屏息凝神想象一个画面——
t_en: Based on your experience, this sound could be transformed into a waveform. You begin to focus, imagining a picture in your mind —

t_ch: 嘿，你，终于醒了。
t_en: Hey, you. You're finally awake.

t_ch: …又是昏迷？
t_en: ...I passed out, again?

t_ch: …这是哪里？我不是…在浴场？
t_en: ...Where am I? Am I... in the bath?

t_ch: …怎么每次醒来都是这句话？
t_en: ...Why do I always say that every time I wake up?

t_ch: 你当时正准备越过悬锋城，对吧？没想到闯进了奥赫玛军队的埋伏，和我们一样。还有旁边这个{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}信徒也是。
t_en: You were just about to cross Castrum Kremnos, right? You didn't expect to get into an ambush by the forces of Okhema, just like us and this believer of {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#} over there.

t_ch: 我不是{RUBY_B#诡计之泰坦}扎格列斯{RUBY_E#}信徒，你也别添乱了。
t_en: I'm not a believer of {RUBY_B#Trickery Titan}Zagreus{RUBY_E#}. Don't make things more complicated.

t_ch: 你不记得发生了什么是吧？喝了「法吉娜盲盒」的人大多都是你这样。
t_en: You don't remember what happened, do you? Just like most people who drink the "Phagousa Blind Box."

t_ch: 你当时叫嚷着「我好想吃红土饲料啊」，就跑出浴场了，十个人也拉不住你。
t_en: You yelled "I want to eat Redsoil Feed!" and ran off the bath. Many people tried to stop you but couldn't hold you back.

t_ch: …我有这么强？
t_en: ...Am I that strong?

t_ch: …我完全没有印象了。
t_en: ...I can't recall any of it.

t_ch: 很正常，人的体质不同。而你曾经就在极度混乱的情况下，在面对全副武装的奥赫玛士兵围剿时全部闪避，趁他们反应过来之前，你就来到这里了。
t_en: It's normal. People's constitutions are different. Even though you were extremely confused, you managed to perfectly evade every attack from a group of fully-armed Okheman soldiers, and before they could react, you ended up here.

t_ch: 你在大地兽面前凶狠偷吃它们的红土，突然站直了「咣」地一下倒在地上…然后大人们没了办法，先把你放在这*大地兽看不见的地方*，等着大地兽消气。
t_en: You stood boldly before the dromases and viciously ate their redsoil, but then suddenly, you straightened up and collapsed to the ground with a loud crash... The adults had no choice but to place you somewhere out of the dromases' sight and wait for them to calm down.

t_ch: 我们俩负责看住你。
t_en: We are in charge of watching over you.

t_ch: …那它现在消气了吗？
t_en: ...Has the dromas calmed down now?

t_ch: 应该没事了。
t_en: It should be fine now.

t_ch: …我宁愿相信我是被传送来的。
t_en: ...I'd rather believe I was teleported here.

t_ch: 没事，你们这样的大人都过得很辛苦的，我理解。
t_en: It's fine. You adults have it tough, I understand.

t_ch: …你们俩都是扎格列斯信徒吧。
t_en: ...You two are both followers of Zagreus, aren't you?

t_ch: 都说了我不是了，你愿意信就信。
t_en: I told you I'm not. Believe what you want.

t_ch: 你走吧，这里已经没有你的事了。
t_en: Well, take your leave then.

t_ch: 一口吨了。
t_en: Down it in one gulp.

t_ch: 眼前一黑。
t_en: Everything goes black.

t_ch: 这一杯秘酿是经过烧制的陶土的味道，粗糙，却又带着几分厚重感，像是树庭的哲学家们会喜欢的味道，也许是哲学家们需要这样的手段激发哲思——
t_en: This ambrosia has the taste of fired clay — rough, yet with a certain heaviness, like something philosophers from the Grove would appreciate. Maybe they need this kind of taste to spark philosophical thought—

t_ch: 你们是谁啊？
t_en: Who are you lot?

t_ch: 我明白了现状了。我有道德洁癖，所以，我是{NICKNAME}的超我。
t_en: I understand the situation now. I have a moral obsession, so I am {NICKNAME}'s superego.

t_ch: 通常来说，{NICKNAME}不会这么快理解现状的。但是既然你这么说了，我愿意相信我自己，我们应该分别是本我，自我，超我。
t_en: Usually, {NICKNAME} wouldn't understand the situation so quickly. But since you said it, I'm willing to believe in myself. We should be the id, ego, and superego.

t_ch: 那么我和{F#她}{M#他}谁是本我，谁是自我？
t_en: So, between me and {F#her}{M#him}, who's the id, and who's the ego?

t_ch: …什么是本我，自我，超我？
t_en: ...What id, ego, and superego?

t_ch: 「本我」代表着原始的冲动和欲望，追求满足避免痛苦，不受社会规范和道德约束。
t_en: "Id" represents primal impulses and desires, seeking to satisfy urges and avoid pain, unrestrained by social norms or morals.

t_ch: 相对的，「超我」代表着道德和规范，遵循「完美原则」，经常和「本我」发生冲突。
t_en: In contrast, the "superego" represents morality and regulations, following the "perfection principle," often in conflict with the "id."

t_ch: 而「自我」就相当于一种平衡，在冲动和自我规范之间协调……
t_en: And the "ego" serves as a balance, mediating between impulses and self-regulation...

t_ch: 我不清楚，另一个我你觉得呢？
t_en: I'm not sure. What do you think, other me?

t_ch: 我感觉我比较纯粹，应该是「本我」。
t_en: I feel like I'm more pure, so I think I'm the "id."

t_ch: 我也很纯粹的，不如说*我们*都很纯粹。
t_en: I'm very pure too. Or rather, we are all very pure.

t_ch: 看起来就我有选项，我是自我。
t_en: Looks like I'm the only one who has options to choose from. I'm the ego.

t_ch: 有道理，但是道理也不多，就当你是「自我」吧。
t_en: Makes sense, but not too much. Fine, treat yourself as the "ego."

t_ch: 这只是个代称，不代表着我真的就是「本我」，你懂吗？不然咱们得叫{NICKNAME}1号，2号，3号了，太蠢了。
t_en: This is just a placeholder, not that I'm really the "id," you know? Otherwise, we'd have to call ourselves {NICKNAME} No.1, No.2, No.3, which would be stupid.

t_ch: 所以机会难得，你有什么想问自己的吗？
t_en: So, this is a rare opportunity — anything you want to ask yourself?

t_ch: {RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的神威持续不了多久，问吧。
t_en: The power of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} won't last long, so ask away.

t_ch: 我究竟是什么性格的开拓者？
t_en: What kind of Trailblazer am I really?

t_ch: 悲观开拓者，热血开拓者，沉默开拓者…这些成就奖励在列车上就能完成，记得拿。
t_en: Pessimistic Trailblazer, Hot-Blooded Trailblazer, Reserved Trailblazer... these Achievements can be completed on the Express. Don't forget to collect them.

t_ch: 你问我，我还问你呢。
t_en: I could ask you the same question.

t_ch: 我的抽卡运气能不能再好些？
t_en: Can I get some better luck in the pulls?

t_ch: 抽卡运气能不能再好一些？我{F#大姐}{M#大哥}问你话呢。
t_en: Can {F#she}{M#he} get some better luck in the pulls? You heard {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 能！如果我说话有用，我确实想说「能」。
t_en: Sure! If my words are useful, I'd say "Sure."

t_ch: 但是我没有那个本事，所以，你应该适度娱乐，理性消费。
t_en: But I don't have that kind of ability, so you should play in moderation and spend rationally.

t_ch: 理性要是有用的话，要警察做什么！
t_en: If rationality really worked, what would we need the police for?

t_ch: …你确实挺「本我」的。「自我」，别听{F#她}{M#他}的。
t_en: ...You're really quite "id"-like. "Ego," don't listen to {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 我自说自话，是不是有点怪？
t_en: Am I talking to myself? Is that weird?

t_ch: 是的。
t_en: Yes.

t_ch: *我*的形象早就崩塌过了，但*我*建议你还是再维护一下。
t_en: My image has already crumbled, but I suggest you try to maintain it a bit more.

t_ch: 喝了「法吉娜盲盒」的人就是这副德行，大家都习惯了，别在意。
t_en: People who drink the "Phagousa Blind Box" usually end up like this. Everyone's used to it. Don't mind it.

t_ch: 不同选项之间差距好像有点大？
t_en: Seems like the options are a bit too different?

t_ch: 这不是说明我的想法比较自由，大家也对我很包容嘛。
t_en: Doesn't that just mean my thoughts are free, and everyone's very tolerant of me?

t_ch: 用符合道德规范的选项铺垫一下，不就是方便我选有病的选项吗？
t_en: Setting things up with morally acceptable options... isn't it just making it easier for me to pick the messed-up ones?

t_ch: 以上仅代表「本我」自己的说法。
t_en: The above is just the "id's" own take.

t_ch: 这么多问题你就让我问一个啊？
t_en: So many questions, and I only get to ask one?

t_ch: 都说了，{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的神威持续不了多久——换句话说，你可以下次问其他的问题。
t_en: I told you, the power of {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} won't last long — so you may save other questions for the next time.

t_ch: 嗨，这不就是自己骗自己，下次还要再喝这什么破盲盒嘛。
t_en: Hey, isn't this just fooling myself? Why should I drink that Blind Box again?

t_ch: 可问题是下次喝了盲盒也不一定是这个效果啊？
t_en: But the Blind Box might not have the same effect next time, right?

t_ch: 真正的问题难道不是，这几个选项也不一定是*我*想问的？
t_en: Isn't the real question that — these options aren't necessarily what I wanted to ask anyway?

t_ch: 别管那么多了。
t_en: Don't think too much about it.

t_ch: 你和自己相谈甚欢。突发奇想，干脆下次让你自己的快乐、担忧、愤怒、讨厌、害怕一起登场如何？可惜咱们没有那么多的颜色，人稍微一多就分不出谁是谁了。
t_en: You chat with yourself in good spirits. A sudden thought crosses your mind — what if next time, all your joy, worry, anger, dislike, and fear all show up at once? Too bad we don't have that many colors, and once there are too many people, it's hard to tell one another.

t_ch: 你清醒了不少，眼前两位自己也随即消失。
t_en: You're much more clear-headed now, and the two versions of yourself before your eyes quickly disappear.

t_ch: 冷静下来一想，你们仨要是分别叫灰宝，灰安，灰宁的…好像也不是不行。
t_en: Thinking it over, you realize if the three of you were named Grayie, Grayanne, and Graynnon... it doesn't sound too bad.

t_ch: 一口吨了。
t_en: Down it in one gulp.

t_ch: 眼前一黑。
t_en: Everything goes black.

t_ch: 这一杯秘酿混入了甜香的果肉，口感绵软，微微带着籽粒，令人回想起站在淋浴头下大嚼水果的感觉。就像是回到了没有蕉绿的过去，人类还不是人类的时候……
t_en: This ambrosia is mixed with sweet and fragrant fruits, with a soft texture and faintly crunchy seeds, bringing to mind the feeling of standing under the shower while munching on fruit. It's like you've traveled back to a time without bananxiety, back when humans weren't quite human yet...

t_ch: …蕉？蕉蕉蕉蕉，蕉蕉……
t_en: Ba? Nana, banabana...

t_ch: 天杀的原始博士还在追我。
t_en: That blasted Dr. Primitive is still chasing me!

t_ch: 翁法罗斯也能有模因污染？
t_en: Can Amphoreus even get memetic pollution?

t_ch: 摆满香蕉的小岛，蕉蕉～
t_en: An island full of bananas! Bananas~

t_ch: 冷静，这并非原始博士所为…这只是借用了你脑海中对于「原始」的想象。
t_en: Calm down. This isn't the work of Dr. Primitive... it's just based on the concept of "primal" in your brain.

t_ch: 如果说一口法吉娜盲盒能让人类变成猴子，你有没有想过猴子再尝一口盲盒会怎么样？
t_en: If drinking the Phagousa Blind Box could turn humans into monkeys, have you ever thought about what would happen if a monkey drinks it?

t_ch: 难、难道说，我还能退化？
t_en: W—Wait... I can regress even further?

t_ch: 进化有无限的可能，这说明了，退化也有……
t_en: Evolution has infinite possibilities, so that means... regression could also...

t_ch: …难不成会变成人类吗？
t_en: ...Could I end up turning back into a human?

t_ch: 法吉娜盲盒可不是什么猴子开关！接着喝下去你只会继续退化……
t_en: The Phagousa Blind Box isn't a monkey switch! If you keep drinking, you'll only keep regressing...

t_ch: 即便是这样，你还敢继续喝吗？
t_en: Even so, do you still dare to drink more?

t_ch: 当然了，超级加倍！
t_en: Of course. I'll double the fun!

t_ch: …退化路线出现了分歧，需要你提前选择。
t_en: ...The regression path has diverged. You need to make a preemptive choice.

t_ch: 退化到空中生活。
t_en: Regress to a life in the sky.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}退化——酒醉的蝴蝶！
t_en: {NICKNAME} regresses — A drunken butterfly!

t_ch: 怎么也飞不出，{RUBY_B#天空之泰坦}艾格勒{RUBY_E#}的天界，原来你是一只，酒醉的蝴蝶。
t_en: You can fly, but you never escape {RUBY_B#Sky Titan}Aquila{RUBY_E#}'s hands. Turns out, you're just a drunken butterfly.

t_ch: 退化到土中生活。
t_en: Regress to a life in the earth.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}退化——奥赫玛的双耳陶瓶！
t_en: {NICKNAME} regresses — an Okhema twin-eared vase!

t_ch: 刚出土的全新文物。
t_en: A freshly unearthed artifact.

t_ch: 退化到水中生活。
t_en: Regress to a life in the water.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}退化——法吉娜圣兽小海豹！
t_en: {NICKNAME} regresses — The baby seal, sacred beast of Phagousa!

t_ch: o((◉ω◉!)o「我就猜到会变成这样」)∋
t_en: O((◉ω◉!)o"I knew this would happen!")∋

t_ch: 退化到故事的开始。
t_en: Regress to the beginning of the story.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}退化——星核！
t_en: {NICKNAME} regresses — a Stellaron!

t_ch: 什么？！星核？！
t_en: What!? A Stellaron!?

t_ch: 幸好！这一切都只是你喝了法吉娜盲盒之后产生的狂想，它不是真的。
t_en: Fortunately, this is all just a wild fantasy after you drank the Phagousa Blind Box. It's not real.

t_ch: 上当了，可恶的法吉娜盲盒，这不是退化，这只是变化！
t_en: I've been tricked! Damn the Phagousa Blind Box. This isn't regression. Just alteration!

t_ch: 对不起，我要退出。
t_en: Sorry, I want out.
